IDM.project_ai_summary:
The service does not currently meet Service Standard 14 (operating a reliable service). Of the 17 repositories assessed, 11 have passed their Quality Gates, while five have failed and one has not yet been assessed. The five failing repositories did not meet new-code conditions, primarily due to insufficient test coverage, unresolved security issues, and high code duplication on new code. The codebase also contains 16 major bugs and 43 major vulnerabilities, along with a high number of code smells (22 critical, 689 major, and 218 minor), indicating reliability and maintainability risks.

Defra Development standards are not consistently met. Five repositories are below the 90% coverage target for new code, and three exceed the 3% duplication limit for new code.

From a governance perspective, seven inactive projects and four stale branches have been identified. A high number of suppressions (752) and false positives (135) have been recorded, with 136 suppressions and 25 false positives lacking justification comments, which weakens auditability.
IDM.path_to_green: Resolve the five Quality Gate failures by addressing coverage gaps, security issues, and code duplication on new code; Work through the 16 major bugs and 43 major vulnerabilities as a priority; Improve test coverage in the five repositories below 90% and reduce duplication in the three repositories above 3% for new code; Add justification comments to the 136 suppressions and 25 false positives that currently lack them; Review and archive the seven inactive projects and remove the four stale branches.

SFI.project_ai_summary:
The service largely aligns with Service Standard 14 (operating a reliable service). Of the five repositories assessed, all have passed their Quality Gates, with no bugs or vulnerabilities identified. A small number of code smells are present, including one major code smell.

All repositories meet Defra Development standards, maintaining 95.2% test coverage and 0.2% code duplication.

From a governance perspective, one false positive and one accepted issue lack justification comments, which is worth addressing for auditability.
SFI.path_to_green: Address the one major code smell to achieve full Green status; Add justification comments to the one false positive and one accepted issue that currently lack them.

Future Grants.project_ai_summary:
The service shows a high level of code quality across its three repositories. All Quality Gates have passed, and there are no bugs, vulnerabilities, or major code smells identified. The service aligns with Service Standard 14 (operating a reliable service).

All repositories meet Defra Development standards, maintaining 96.2% test coverage and 0.2% code duplication.

From a governance perspective, 18 false positives have been recorded, of which 17 lack justification comments, and ten accepted issues have been identified, of which seven lack justification comments. Adding rationale to these decisions would strengthen auditability.

Single Front Door.project_ai_summary:
The service shows excellent code quality across all ten repositories. All Quality Gates have passed, and there are no bugs, vulnerabilities, or code smells identified. The service aligns with Service Standard 14 (operating a reliable service).

All repositories meet Defra Development standards, maintaining 95.8% test coverage and 0.1% code duplication.

From a governance perspective, 27 accepted issues have been recorded, none of which include a justification comment, and four of eight false positives also lack comments. Adding rationale to these suppression decisions would improve auditability across the service.

Animal Health & Wealth.project_ai_summary:
The service does not currently meet Service Standard 14 (operating a reliable service). Of the 19 repositories assessed, all have passed their Quality Gates; however, the codebase contains a number of high-severity code smells, including 3 blocker and 8 critical code smells alongside 90 major code smells, indicating significant maintainability risks. There are no bugs or vulnerabilities identified.

Defra Development standards are not consistently met. One repository is below the 90% coverage target for new code.

From a governance perspective, one inactive project has been identified, and three accepted issues lack justification comments.
Animal Health & Wealth.path_to_green: Resolve the 3 blocker and 8 critical code smells as a priority, then work through the 90 major code smells to reduce technical debt; Improve test coverage in the one repository below 90% for new code; Review and archive the inactive project and add justification comments to the three accepted issues that currently lack them.
